title: Sesquicella papaver

The Cistaceae family is a part of the order Malvales, and the Malvales order is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Malvaceae, Bixaceae, and Celastraceae. Sesquicella papaver is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its distinctive papery flowers and succulent leaves. The classification of Sesquicella papaver is further divided into its species, which are characterized by their unique morphological and anatomical features. The classification of Sesquicella papaver is an ongoing area of research in botany and taxonomy.
